drugs.notes: Infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride at maximum rate of 1mg cisplatin/min (minimum 120 minutes). Administered 30 minutes after end of pemetrexed infusion. Cycles 1-4 only.
Infusion in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes using a low protein binding 0.2 micron filter. Administered before cisplatin and pemetrexed. All 35 cycles. Dose reductions NOT permitted.

Infusion in 100ml glucose 5% or sodium chloride 0.9% over 10 minutes. All 35 cycles (maintenance-only cycles 5-35 without cisplatin). Consideration should be given to draining pleural or peritoneal effusions prior to administration.

dose_modifications.notes: Consider dose reduction if CrCl falls below 45ml/min

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: Platelets <50 with NCI-CTC grade 2 bleeding
Haematological: Platelets <50 with NCI-CTC grade 2 bleeding
Pembrolizumab: NCI-CTC grade 3-4 pneumonitis, nephritis, infusion reaction or grade 4 adverse reaction
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue pembrolizumab. Dose reductions not permitted.
Withhold and administer corticosteroids; may resume when liver function at NCI-CTC grade 1 following steroid taper

dose_modifications.condition: Pembrolizumab: Hepatic injury AST/ALT >=5xULN or bilirubin >3xULN

dose_modifications.notes: Withhold and administer corticosteroids; may resume when reaction at NCI-CTC grade 1 or below
Current funding approval precludes further treatment after interruption of 12 weeks or longer

pre_medications: Folic acid 5mg once daily oral - starting 1-2 weeks prior to and continuing for 3 weeks after last dose of pemetrexed
Hydroxocobalamin 1mg IM injection every 3 months - starting 1-2 weeks prior to pemetrexed
Dexamethasone 4mg twice daily oral - starting day before chemotherapy (skin rash prophylaxis for pemetrexed); if forgotten administer dexamethasone 20mg IV 15-30 min before chemotherapy
Aprepitant 125mg oral - 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy (cycles 1-4)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV - 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy (cycles 1-4)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV - 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy (cycles 5-35)
Furosemide 40mg oral or IV - pre-hydration (cycles 1-4 with cisplatin)
1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride and 16mmol magnesium sulphate IV over 60 minutes - pre-hydration (cycles 1-4)
Chlorphenamine 10mg IV - as required for infusion related reactions
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV - as required for infusion related reactions
Paracetamol 1000mg oral - as required for infusion related reactions
1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride and 16mmol magnesium sulphate IV over 60 minutes - post-hydration (cycles 1-4)
special_warnings: Pembrolizumab dose reductions are NOT permitted; treatment may only be interrupted or discontinued
Autoimmune toxicities associated with pembrolizumab can be life-threatening; monitor closely
Immune-related adverse reactions may involve GI, liver, skin, nervous, endocrine or other organ systems
Cisplatin: full hydration protocol required - pre and post hydration with electrolyte replacement
Patients must drink at least 3 litres of fluid in the 24 hours after cisplatin administration
Cisplatin: nephrotoxicity, neuropathy, ototoxicity risk - monitor renal function, consider formal audiology
Pembrolizumab: risk of pneumonitis, nephritis, colitis, thyroid disorders, hypophysitis, hepatitis
PD-L1 testing must be attempted prior to treatment start
EGFR and ALK mutation status must be confirmed negative prior to treatment
Treatment is 4 cycles of combination therapy followed by 31 cycles of maintenance pembrolizumab plus pemetrexed (total 35 cycles / 2 years)
Treatment breaks up to 12 weeks allowed for immune toxicities only; funding may be lost after 12-week interruption
Pemetrexed: consider draining pleural or peritoneal effusions prior to administration
If third dose reduction necessary, stop treatment
EGFR and ALK mutation negative disease required for pembrolizumab eligibility

drugs.notes: Infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride at maximum rate of 1mg cisplatin/min (minimum 120 minutes)
Infusion in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes. Day 8 dose subject to haematological dose modifications.

dose_modifications.notes: Consider dose adjustments if CrCl less than 30ml/min
Delay treatment for 7 days. If counts recover continue at initial dose.
Omit dose. Do not reinstate until neutrophils >=0.5x10^9/L
Omit dose. Do not reinstate until platelets >=50x10^9/L

Gemcitabine: radiosensitising properties - caution if concurrent or prior radiotherapy
Gemcitabine: risk of pneumonitis - monitor respiratory symptoms
Day 8 gemcitabine dose subject to haematological assessment on day 8

Indications: NSCLC (non-adenocarcinoma) and thymic carcinoma (squamous cell)

drugs.notes: 250mg twice daily, continuous. Swallow whole, do not chew or crush. Available as 200mg and 250mg capsules. Continuous dosing throughout 28-day cycles.

dose_modifications.notes: Continue at same dose; monitor as clinically indicated
Interrupt until toxicity reduces to grade 2 or below, then restart at same dose

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological NCI-CTC grade 4 (first or second occurrence)
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt until toxicity reduces to grade 2 or below, then restart at lower dose
Interrupt until toxicity reduces to grade 1 or baseline; restart at lower dose. If recurrence, discontinue. Third recurrence: discontinue permanently.

dose_modifications.condition: AST or ALT >3xULN and bilirubin >1.5xULN (without cholestasis or hemolysis)

dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt until toxicity reduces to grade 1 or below, then restart at lower dose
Permanently discontinue crizotinib in patients diagnosed with treatment-related pneumonitis
No dosing recommendation available - insufficient data
Use with extra caution - not well studied
Not recommended in severe hepatic impairment

pre_medications: Loperamide 4mg oral stat after first loose stool, then 2-4mg as required for diarrhoea (maximum 16mg/24 hours)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral three times daily as required for nausea and vomiting
special_warnings: ALK-positive NSCLC only - ALK fusion status must be confirmed positive prior to treatment
Second or subsequent line treatment post first line combination chemotherapy
QTc prolongation risk - monitor ECG; use with caution in patients with history of QTc prolongation, predisposition, or taking QT-prolonging medicines
Hepatotoxicity risk - monitor LFTs every 2 weeks for first 2 months then monthly; crizotinib can cause liver abnormalities
Severe, life-threatening or fatal pneumonitis/ILD risk - all cases occurred within 2 months of initiation; permanently discontinue if treatment-related pneumonitis confirmed
Interstitial lung disease risk - monitor for pulmonary symptoms indicative of pneumonitis
Crizotinib interacts with a number of other medications
National Patient Safety Alert on oral chemotherapy (NPSA/2008/RRR001) must be followed
Capsules must be swallowed whole - do not crush or chew
GI perforation risk
Eye disorders risk
This protocol may require funding approval

drugs.notes: 50mg twice daily for 14 days (28-day cycle). ALTERNATIVE: 200mg once daily for 5 days (21-day cycle). Available as 50mg tablets. Doses must be rounded to nearest 50mg or alternate day dosing used. Take on empty stomach (1 hour before food), swallow whole with plenty of water.

50mg twice daily for 14 days (28-day cycle). ALTERNATIVE: 200mg once daily for 5 days (21-day cycle). Available as 50mg and 100mg capsules. Take on empty stomach (1 hour before food). Oral chemotherapy - short course only, must not be continued beyond prescribed days. Prescribe from secondary care only.

dose_modifications.condition: Etoposide: Bilirubin 26-51 micromol/L or AST 60-180 units/L
Etoposide: Bilirubin >51 micromol/L or AST >180 units/L

dose_modifications.notes: Delay for 7 days. If counts recover continue at initial dose.

pre_medications: Metoclopramide 10mg oral three times daily as required
Allopurinol - for patients at risk of tumour lysis syndrome: begin day before chemotherapy and continue while significant chemosensitive tumour bulk remains (normally one cycle)
special_warnings: SMALL CELL LUNG CANCER (SCLC) protocol - NOT for NSCLC
ORAL CHEMOTHERAPY - National Patient Safety Agency Alert NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be adhered to
Short course therapy only - must not be continued beyond prescribed days
Must be prescribed from secondary care only
Cyclophosphamide: haemorrhagic cystitis risk - ensure adequate hydration; consider mesna if renal impairment
SCLC can be very sensitive to chemotherapy - risk of tumour lysis syndrome at start of therapy; prescribe allopurinol for at-risk patients
Two regimen options available: 28-day cycle (50mg BD x14 days) or 21-day cycle (200mg OD x5 days)
Doses must be rounded to nearest 50mg or alternate day dosing used to facilitate correct dose
Etoposide and cyclophosphamide capsules/tablets available as 50mg and 100mg/50mg respectively
If third dose reduction necessary, stop treatment
